sentences.quote: Phase~6m closes the Sakharov 4-criterion induced-gravity test introduced in Phase~6e, providing the first systematic $\Lambda_J$ vs $\Lambda_{\rm HK}$ comparison on a common substrate in the literature.
sentences.quote_normalized: phase~6m closes the sakharov 4-criterion induced-gravity test introduced in phase~6e, providing the first systematic $\lambda_j$ vs $\lambda_{\rm hk}$ comparison on a common substrate in the literature.
sentences.agent_notes: First-claim verifiability: framed as 'first systematic Lambda_J vs Lambda_HK comparison on a common substrate' is defensible — the deep-research return (Wave 4a) finds no primary source that constructs the per-substrate Lambda_J vs Lambda_HK comparison explicitly with the 4-condition Sakharov criterion as the discriminant. FLS 2012 PRL/Proc and BLM 2014 publish the BEC-side Lambda_J finding alone; Jannes-Volovik 2012 publishes the He3-A side alone; no primary source pairs them as 'one substrate satisfies the 4-criterion and gives Lambda_J=Lambda_HK / another fails (ii) and gives the depletion-suppressed inequality.' Wave 4a.4 verdict (B) does not weaken the first-claim status — it only retires the (<=) biconditional reading. WARN (not PASS) because the claim is appropriate ONLY when the 'common substrate' phrasing is read as 'common Sakharov-criterion framework applied across substrates' — not 'a single shared substrate' (which would not be true since two different substrates He3-A and FLS BEC are compared). Sentence reads correctly under the intended meaning, but the phrasing 'on a common substrate' is mildly ambiguous (depletion-factor finding does live on FLS BEC alone). Recommendation: consider tightening to 'on a common comparison framework' or 'across emergent-gravity substrates' in a future polish pass; not blocking.

sentences.quote: The substantive (\,$\Rightarrow$\,) implication at the $\mathbb{R}$ level is captured in JTGR12 (\texttt{sakharov\_criterion\_implies\_lambda\_j\_eq\_lambda\_hk\_real}); the ${}^3\!\text{He-A}$ extended substrate witness satisfies $\lambda_J = \lambda_{\rm HK}$ at $\mathbb{R}$ level (JTGR13, \texttt{volovikJannes\_he3a\_lambda\_j\_eq\_lambda\_hk\_real}); and the FLS BEC extended substrate witness structurally separates $\lambda_J \neq \lambda_{\rm HK}$ at $\mathbb{R}$ level (JTGR14, \texttt{flsBEC\_lambda\_j\_neq\_lambda\_hk\_real}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the substantive (\,$\rightarrow$\,) implication at the $\mathbb{r}$ level is captured in jtgr12 (sakharov\_criterion\_implies\_lambda\_j\_eq\_lambda\_hk\_real); the ${}^3\!he-a$ extended substrate witness satisfies $\lambda_j = \lambda_{\rm hk}$ at $\mathbb{r}$ level (jtgr13, volovikjannes\_he3a\_lambda\_j\_eq\_lambda\_hk\_real); and the fls bec extended substrate witness structurally separates $\lambda_j \neq \lambda_{\rm hk}$ at $\mathbb{r}$ level (jtgr14, flsbec\_lambda\_j\_neq\_lambda\_hk\_real).
sentences.agent_notes: TN check: all three Lean theorem references (JTGR12, JTGR13, JTGR14) resolve to existing fully-qualified names in lean/lean_deps.json. JTGR12 = sakharov_criterion_implies_lambda_j_eq_lambda_hk_real (line 649 of JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean); JTGR13 = volovikJannes_he3a_lambda_j_eq_lambda_hk_real (line 663); JTGR14 = flsBEC_lambda_j_neq_lambda_hk_real (line 679). All three are substantive (>=4-line proofs).

sentences.quote: The deep-research return at \texttt{Lit-Search/Phase-6o/6o-Wave 4a FLS BEC Depletion-Factor $\Lambda$\_substrate Return.md} (returned 2026-05-06) closes the Wave~4a.4 deferred deliverable with verdict~(B): the (\,$\Leftarrow$\,)~biconditional is \emph{honestly retired} in favour of a one-way implication $\text{Sakharov 4-criterion} \Rightarrow \Lambda_J = \Lambda_{\rm HK}$ paired with a substantively-deeper substrate-physics encoding.
sentences.quote_normalized: the deep-research return at lit-search/phase-6o/6o-wave 4a fls bec depletion-factor $\lambda$\_substrate return.md (returned 2026-05-06) closes the wave~4a.4 deferred deliverable with verdict~(b): the (\,$\leftarrow$\,)~biconditional is honestly retired in favour of a one-way implication $sakharov 4-criterion \rightarrow \lambda_j = \lambda_{\rm hk}$ paired with a substantively-deeper substrate-physics encoding.

Verdict (B) faithfully reproduced from §6 of that file: 'Honestly retire the biconditional reading and ship as a one-way implication Sakharov 4-criterion ⇒ Λ_J = Λ_HK, with deeper substrate encoding (the depletion pre-factor as a substrate-side computable real number) for transparency.' The prose framing ('honestly retired', 'substantively-deeper substrate-physics encoding') matches the verdict (B) language exactly.

sentences.quote: Primary-source coverage of (\,$\Leftarrow$\,) is asymmetric: Volovik--Jannes 2012 \cite{JannesVolovik2012} (JETP Lett.\ 96, 215, \S\,VII) argues only \emph{forward}, from Atiyah--Bott-- Shapiro topological protection at Weyl points to universal coupling and the equality $\Lambda_J \propto \Delta_0^4$ on $^3\!\text{He-A}$; no primary source argues the converse.
sentences.quote_normalized: primary-source coverage of (\,$\leftarrow$\,) is asymmetric: volovik--jannes 2012 (jetp lett.\ 96, 215, \s\,vii) argues only forward, from atiyah--bott-- shapiro topological protection at weyl points to universal coupling and the equality $\lambda_j \propto \delta_0^4$ on $^3\!he-a$; no primary source argues the converse.
sentences.agent_notes: JannesVolovik2012 entry exists in CITATION_REGISTRY with primary-source-cached PDF at Lit-Search/Phase-6m/primary-sources/JannesVolovik2012.pdf (PDF found at the project-root Lit-Search via find). Citation reference matches §VII of the JETP Lett. 96, 215 paper (arXiv:1108.5086). Asymmetry-claim (forward only, no converse) reproduces deep-research return §6 verdict.

sentences.quote: The Finazzi--Liberati--Sindoni acoustic-BEC analogue \cite{FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012PRL, FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012Proc} provides an explicit substrate where universal coupling fails (depletion-sector atoms do not propagate on the BEC effective metric) \emph{and} $\Lambda_J \neq \Lambda_{\rm HK}$ by the structurally enforced FLS Eq.~(71) depletion factor $\sqrt{\rho_0 a^3}$, but this only confirms the forward implication; it does not test (\,$\Leftarrow$\,).
sentences.quote_normalized: the finazzi--liberati--sindoni acoustic-bec analogue provides an explicit substrate where universal coupling fails (depletion-sector atoms do not propagate on the bec effective metric) and $\lambda_j \neq \lambda_{\rm hk}$ by the structurally enforced fls eq.~(71) depletion factor $\sqrt{\rho_0 a^3}$, but this only confirms the forward implication; it does not test (\,$\leftarrow$\,).
sentences.agent_notes: Both citation registry entries verified: (i) FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012PRL, doi=10.1103/PhysRevLett.108.071101, arxiv=1103.4841, primary-source PDF cached at Lit-Search/Phase-1-and-Background/primary-sources/FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012PRL.pdf (verified by find). (ii) FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012Proc, arxiv=1204.3039, doi=None (proceedings, no DOI), primary-source PDF cached. Eq. 71 reference matches deep-research return §3 (Eq. 71 = load-bearing depletion-factor closed form Λ_FLS proportional to √(ρ_0 a^3)).

sentences.quote: The depletion-factor finding is independently corroborated by the relativistic-BEC extension of Belenchia--Liberati--Mohd \cite{BelenchiaLiberatiMohd2014}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the depletion-factor finding is independently corroborated by the relativistic-bec extension of belenchia--liberati--mohd .

Independent-corroboration framing matches deep-research return §1.

sentences.quote: The Wave~4a.4 closure ships the depletion factor as a load-bearing $\mathbb{R}$-valued substrate-physics field on \texttt{SakharovExtended} with the unconditional relation $\Lambda_J = \texttt{depletion} \cdot \Lambda_{\rm HK}$ (JTGR16, \texttt{sakharov\_depletion\_factor\_relation}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the wave~4a.4 closure ships the depletion factor as a load-bearing $\mathbb{r}$-valued substrate-physics field on sakharovextended with the unconditional relation $\lambda_j = depletion \cdot \lambda_{\rm hk}$ (jtgr16, sakharov\_depletion\_factor\_relation).
sentences.agent_notes: TN check: JTGR16 = sakharov_depletion_factor_relation resolves in lean_deps.json (full name: SKEFTHawking.JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.SakharovExtended.sakharov_depletion_factor_relation, JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean line 707). Theorem statement matches prose exactly: 'S.lambdaJ = S.depletion * S.lambdaHK' for any S : SakharovExtended (unconditional). Discharged by S.depletionRelation field which is constructor-required. Sentence's load-bearing prose framing is faithful.

sentences.quote: On $^3\!\text{He-A}$ the depletion factor is exactly~$1$ (JTGR17, \texttt{volovikJannes\_he3a\_depletion\_eq\_one}), reflecting universal coupling at the topologically protected Weyl point.
sentences.quote_normalized: on $^3\!he-a$ the depletion factor is exactly~$1$ (jtgr17, volovikjannes\_he3a\_depletion\_eq\_one), reflecting universal coupling at the topologically protected weyl point.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: JTGR17 resolves; theorem proof is `rfl` discharging `volovikJannes_he3a_extended.depletion = 1` (def-level identity, line 611 of JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean: depletion := 1). 'Reflecting universal coupling at the topologically protected Weyl point' matches Jannes-Volovik 2012 §III narrative (Atiyah-Bott-Shapiro topological reason).

sentences.quote: On FLS BEC the depletion factor satisfies $0 < \texttt{depletion} < 1$ structurally (JTGR18, \texttt{flsBEC\_depletion\_strictly\_between\_zero\_and\_one}) with placeholder $\sqrt{\rho_0 a^3} \approx 8 \times 10^{-3}$ for canonical Steinhauer-class $^{87}\text{Rb}$ parameters \cite{FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012Proc} (Eq.~67/71); the FLS p.~16 domain-of-validity constraint $\rho_0 a^3 \ll 1$ enforces this strict-inequality bound non-tunably.
sentences.quote_normalized: on fls bec the depletion factor satisfies $0 < depletion < 1$ structurally (jtgr18, flsbec\_depletion\_strictly\_between\_zero\_and\_one) with placeholder $\sqrt{\rho_0 a^3} \approx 8 \times 10^{-3}$ for canonical steinhauer-class $^{87}rb$ parameters (eq.~67/71); the fls p.~16 domain-of-validity constraint $\rho_0 a^3 \ll 1$ enforces this strict-inequality bound non-tunably.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: JTGR18 resolves. Theorem proves `0 < flsBEC_extended.depletion ∧ flsBEC_extended.depletion < 1` via `unfold flsBEC_extended; refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩ <;> norm_num` against `depletion := 8.0e-3` (line 633). Placeholder value 8e-3 matches deep-research return §3.3 numerics: na^3 ≈ 7e-5 -> sqrt(na^3) ≈ 8.4e-3 (within 5% — placeholder rounded to 8e-3). FLS p.16 domain-of-validity constraint correctly cited from FinazziLiberatiSindoni2012Proc (arXiv:1204.3039).

sentences.quote: The $^3\!\text{He-A}$ vs.\ FLS~BEC depletion-factor asymmetry (JTGR19, \texttt{volovikJannes\_vs\_flsBEC\_depletion\_asymmetry}) is the load-bearing $\mathbb{R}$-valued substrate distinction the prior Boolean 4-criterion did not capture.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $^3\!he-a$ vs.\ fls~bec depletion-factor asymmetry (jtgr19, volovikjannes\_vs\_flsbec\_depletion\_asymmetry) is the load-bearing $\mathbb{r}$-valued substrate distinction the prior boolean 4-criterion did not capture.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: JTGR19 resolves at JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean line 751. Theorem proves `volovikJannes_he3a_extended.depletion ≠ flsBEC_extended.depletion`, discharged by rewriting via JTGR17 (= 1) then unfolding flsBEC_extended (depletion = 8e-3) and `norm_num` (1 ≠ 8e-3). 'Load-bearing R-valued substrate distinction' framing is accurate: prior Boolean SakharovConditions.lambdaEffEqLambdaHK is `false` for FLS BEC and `true` for He3-A but does not encode the magnitude of the deviation; the depletion field does.

sentences.quote: The composed honest-one-way closure (JTGR20, \texttt{wave\_4a\_4\_honest\_one\_way\_closure}) packages the (\,$\Rightarrow$\,) implication, the unconditional depletion-factor relation, the $^3\!\text{He-A}$ unit-depletion identity, and the FLS BEC strict-bound asymmetry into a single substantive theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: the composed honest-one-way closure (jtgr20, wave\_4a\_4\_honest\_one\_way\_closure) packages the (\,$\rightarrow$\,) implication, the unconditional depletion-factor relation, the $^3\!he-a$ unit-depletion identity, and the fls bec strict-bound asymmetry into a single substantive theorem.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: JTGR20 resolves at line 773. The theorem packages exactly the four conjuncts the prose enumerates: (i) sakharov_criterion_implies_lambda_j_eq_lambda_hk_real (the (=>) implication, JTGR12), (ii) sakharov_depletion_factor_relation (unconditional depletion-factor relation, JTGR16), (iii) volovikJannes_he3a_depletion_eq_one (He3-A unit-depletion identity, JTGR17), (iv) flsBEC_depletion_strictly_between_zero_and_one (FLS BEC strict-bound asymmetry, JTGR18). Single substantive theorem proved by anonymous-constructor; no axioms, no sorry. Prose framing 'single substantive theorem' is exact (composition is structural, not just a docstring grouping).

sentences.quote: The substrate-physics encoding is now load-bearing; the prior P5-anti-pattern Boolean projection of $\lambda_{\rm Eff}\!=\!\lambda_{\rm HK}$ has been strictly-extended to a witness-required $\mathbb{R}$-level identity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the substrate-physics encoding is now load-bearing; the prior p5-anti-pattern boolean projection of $\lambda_{\rm eff}\!=\!\lambda_{\rm hk}$ has been strictly-extended to a witness-required $\mathbb{r}$-level identity.
sentences.agent_notes: P5-anti-pattern reference is the project's preemptive-strengthening-discipline §5 in CLAUDE.md (defining-the-conclusion check). The `SakharovExtended` structure constructor genuinely requires a witness-equality Prop field `lambdaJEqLambdaHKEvidence` (line 587-588) AND a depletionRelation Prop field (line 596) — both must be supplied at construction. This grounds the strict-extension claim. The P5-anti-pattern was the prior Boolean `lambdaEffEqLambdaHK` field (now retained but no longer load-bearing for the R-level identity).

sentences.section: §11.5 Sakharov Lambda_J vs Lambda_HK (embedded short note)
sentences.quote: The Sakharov 4-criterion test (\S\ref{sec:phase6m-sakharov}) is the first systematic $\Lambda$-comparison on a common substrate in the literature, and constitutes a publishable observation in its own right.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sakharov 4-criterion test (\s) is the first systematic $\lambda$-comparison on a common substrate in the literature, and constitutes a publishable observation in its own right.
sentences.agent_notes: Same 'first systematic Λ-comparison on a common substrate in the literature' first-claim framing as line 985. Verdict (B) honest retirement DOES NOT undermine first-claim status — it only retires the (<=) biconditional reading; the systematic per-substrate comparison via the 4-criterion-driven framework remains a publication-novel observation. WARN (not PASS) because: (a) ambiguous 'common substrate' phrasing — the comparison is across two substrates (He3-A and FLS BEC), not on one common substrate, so 'common comparison framework' would be tighter; and (b) the §11.5 short-note also says 'publishable observation in its own right' which now has slightly weaker grounds post-verdict-(B) than before, since the substantive result is now 'one-way implication + load-bearing depletion factor' rather than a biconditional. Recommendation: future polish pass tighten phrasing to 'first systematic Sakharov-criterion-driven Λ_J vs Λ_HK comparison across emergent-gravity substrates, with one substrate (³He-A) satisfying the criterion and one (FLS BEC) failing it'.

sentences.quote: The Phase~6e Sakharov-criterion cross-bridge (§\ref{sec:phase6m-sakharov}) provides the program's first systematic $\Lambda_J$ vs $\Lambda_{\rm HK}$ test on a common substrate, falsifying acoustic-BEC analog gravity at condition~(ii).
sentences.quote_normalized: the phase~6e sakharov-criterion cross-bridge (§) provides the programs first systematic $\lambda_j$ vs $\lambda_{\rm hk}$ test on a common substrate, falsifying acoustic-bec analog gravity at condition~(ii).
sentences.agent_notes: Same first-systematic Λ_J vs Λ_HK framing as line 985 + 1109. WARN: 'falsifying acoustic-BEC analog gravity at condition (ii)' is fine — JTGR9 (`fls_bec_violates_universal_coupling`) discharges this. But 'on a common substrate' phrasing is again mildly ambiguous (the test is across substrates, not on one common substrate). Three-place repetition (lines 985, 1109, 1209) of this exact ambiguous framing is a minor consistency issue — recommend a single tightened phrasing across all three locations in a future polish pass. Not blocking.
